Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I need to remember what an accepting Petri net needs a Petri net initial marking final marking right And were using pm4pys classes so I need to import the necessary stuff first

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First lets list out all the elements from the scenario First the activities are C CR DV RDS the re document step FC II in depth investigation CA AP P N CL closure end Wait right C is the incoming claim so thats the initial transition Wait no wait places are states transitions are activities Oh right transitions are the activities places are the states between them

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So C is the first activity then CR Wait wait no wait maybe I misread Wait no lets check again A customer files an Insurance Claim C after which the following steps take place 1 Claim Registration CR Oh so C is the submission of the claim then CR is logging it

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Lets start with the initial marking theres a start place lets call it start which has 1 token initially representing a new claim arriving

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If docs are incomplete then RDS customer re submits docs then you go back to DV to verify again So thats a loop DV RDS DV

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If the Fraud Check FC raises doubts an additional In depth Investigation II is performed This can happen multiple times before proceeding to Claim Assessment CA So sequence FC if doubts II FC if ok CA So thats a loop between FC and II

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Approval AP the claim must be approved by a senior manager if the amount surpasses a certain threshold So that means after CA if the amount is over threshold we need to do AP then go to P If not we go directly to P

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: After CA we have p after CA From there two transitions one is AP Approval and the other is a silent transition for no approval needed

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait lets rework the places with that in mind after CR we go directly to DV so input to DV is p after CR output of DV is p after DV from p after DV two options either go to FC or go to RDS

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: I made a mistake earlier t rds input is p after dv not p after cr and t rds output is p after cr because after RDS we need to do DV again

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: When RDS fires it puts the token back into p after cr so DV can fire again That handles multiple RDS steps

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: When II fires it takes token from p after fc and puts it into p after dv so then FC can fire again That allows multiple II steps

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lets list all transitions we need labeled with the activity names Insurance Claim Claim Registration Document Verification Re Document Submission Fraud Check In depth Investigation Claim Assessment Approval Payment Notification Closure

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Lets list all the places we need p start p after claim p after cr p after dv p after fc p after ca p before payment p after payment p after notification p closed

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: From p after dv there are arcs to t fc and t rds From p after fc there are arcs to t ii and t ca From p after ca there are arcs to t ap and t skip ap

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: t rds output is p after cr back to before dv and t ii output is p after dv back to before fc

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Create the PetriNet instance create all places create all transitions add all places to net places add all transitions to net transitions then add all the arcs

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets walk through a sample trace no loops approval needed C CR DV FC CA AP P N CL and it reaches p closed which is the final marking

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Sample trace with one RDS loop no approval C CR DV RDS DV FC CA skip AP P N CL ends at p closed Correct

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Sample trace with two II loops approval needed C CR DV FC II FC II FC CA AP P N CL and it also ends at p closed

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Initial marking im is Marking with p start equal to 1 Final marking fm is Marking with p closed equal to 1
